title: Search open orders for warehouse staff
summary: STRUCTURAL EXAMPLE — query slice type. This file demonstrates the structural shape of a query slice, including a read model with source-to-field mapping, freshness policy, cursor-based pagination, and empty-result handling. The domain content is fictional and must not be copied.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Warehouse staff member assigned to a fulfilment zone
stakeholder_intent.goal: Find open orders in their zone so they can begin picking without navigating unrelated orders
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Reduces order processing time by surfacing only actionable orders; replaces a full-table dump that warehouse staff previously filtered manually
stakeholder_intent.trigger: A warehouse staff member opens the order queue dashboard
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A paginated, zone-filtered list of open orders is returned within 200 ms

in_scope: Return a paginated list of open orders visible to the requesting staff member
Filter by fulfilment zone and order status subset (OPEN, PICKING)

preconditions: The requester is an authenticated warehouse staff member
The fulfilment zone identifier is valid and assigned to the requester

business_rules.rule: Only orders whose zone matches the staff member's assigned zone are returned
business_rules.outcome: Zone-filtered order list is returned; no cross-zone data leaks

business_rules.rule: Cursor-based pagination is applied with a page size capped at 50
business_rules.applies_when: Applying pagination to the result set
business_rules.outcome: Clients receive a stable, bounded page and an opaque cursor for the next page

inputs.name: zone
constraints: Must match the staff member's assigned zone

inputs.name: cursor
constraints: Opaque cursor returned by a previous response; omit on first page

outputs.body: JSON object with items array of OpenOrderSummary and optional nextCursor string

errors.condition: Staff member requests a zone they are not assigned to
errors.message: Zone is not assigned to the requesting staff member

errors.condition: Cursor value is malformed or has expired
errors.message: Pagination cursor is invalid or has expired

read_models.name: OpenOrderSummary
read_models.purpose: Provides a compact, zone-filtered view of open orders for the warehouse queue dashboard

fields.name: customerReference
fields.meaning: Human-readable customer reference code printed on the pick slip

fields.name: status
fields.meaning: Current lifecycle status; only OPEN and PICKING are returned by this query

fields.name: lineCount
fields.source: COUNT(*) from order_lines where order_id = orders.id
fields.meaning: Number of distinct line items in the order; used for pick complexity estimation

fields.name: createdAt
fields.meaning: Order creation timestamp; primary sort key (descending)

fields.name: zone
fields.meaning: Fulfilment zone the order is assigned to

read_models.freshness: Read from the primary replica with a replication lag of at most 2 seconds; staleness is acceptable because warehouse staff refresh the view manually
read_models.filtering_sorting_pagination: Filter by orders.fulfilment_zone = :zone and orders.status IN ('OPEN','PICKING'); sort by orders.created_at DESC; cursor encodes last seen (created_at, id) pair; page size capped at 50
read_models.empty_result: Return HTTP 200 with an empty items array and no nextCursor field; do not return 404
read_models.authorization_filtering: Reject requests where the authenticated staff member's assigned_zone does not equal the requested zone parameter; apply the check before executing the database query

security.authentication: Validate the warehouse staff session token from the Authorization header using the existing WarehouseAuthMiddleware
authorization_rules: The authenticated staff member's assigned zone must equal the requested zone parameter
security.data_classification: Internal operational data; does not contain customer PII

architecture_constraints: HTTP handlers call query use cases and never access repositories directly
allowed_changes: Add the open-orders query handler, use case, and read model projector
forbidden_changes: Do not add any write path or domain event publication
dependencies: Reuse the existing CursorCodec from the inventory query module
implementation_context.migration_strategy: No schema migration required; orders and order_lines tables already contain all projected fields
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Read-only query against the replica; no locking or concurrency control required

acceptance_scenarios.title: Staff member retrieves first page of open orders for their zone
given: An authenticated warehouse staff member is assigned to zone NORTH
Three open orders exist in zone NORTH and two in zone SOUTH
then: The response contains three orders all with zone NORTH
Orders are sorted by createdAt descending
No orders from zone SOUTH appear

acceptance_scenarios.title: Staff member is denied access to a zone they are not assigned to
given: An authenticated warehouse staff member is assigned to zone NORTH
then: The response is HTTP 403 with code ZONE_ACCESS_DENIED

test_cases: Unit-test zone authorization check for matching and non-matching zone
Unit-test cursor encoding and decoding round-trip
Integration-test paginated result against a seeded replica database
non_functional_requirements: Response time must be under 200 ms at the 99th percentile under normal warehouse load
definition_of_done: No write path or domain event is introduced

decision_log.decision: Use cursor-based pagination instead of offset-based pagination
decision_log.rationale: The inventory query module already provides CursorCodec; offset pagination produces inconsistent results when orders are inserted concurrently during a page fetch

decision_log.decision: Read from the primary replica with up to 2 seconds of replication lag
decision_log.rationale: Warehouse staff refresh the view manually; sub-second freshness is not required and replica reads reduce load on the primary

codebase_evidence.finding: CursorCodec encodes and decodes opaque pagination cursors using a (created_at, id) pair; already used by the inventory listing query and confirmed reusable
